mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month through discounted cash flow repricing.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDPC1 acceleration over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.5%, markets price in tighter liquidity conditions, compressing equity multiples and driving negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and rising payroll employment growth signal labor market overheating, triggering inflation fears that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of UNRATE and PAYEMS changes fails to predict SPX 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, sustained industrial production growth reflects capacity utilization improvements that drive positive quarterly equity returns as earnings catch up to supply expansion.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher during periods of positive INDPRO momentum compared to contraction periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll growth exceeding the historical median directly boosts consumer spending expectations, providing immediate positive momentum to equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 500,000 units, it signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns do not decline significantly when HOUST drops below 500 for three consecutive months compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations combined with stagnant industrial output create stagflationary pricing pressure, forcing discount rates higher while earnings growth stalls, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and INDPRO levels fails to explain SPX 20-day return variance beyond single-factor models (incremental R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid real GDP acceleration, corporate profit margins expand faster than valuation multiples contract, generating strong positive weekly equity returns driven by earnings revisions.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with rising credit demand
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no positive relationship with GDPC1 acceleration when RSAFS growth exceeds its 12-month median (Rank IC < 0.03).
